name: skyll
description: REST API and MCP server enabling AI agents to search and learn agent skills at runtime. Aggregates skills from multiple sources, fetches SKILL.md content from GitHub, and returns structured JSON for context injection. Published on PyPI. Supports Claude Code, Cursor, and other frameworks.

findings_summary.notes: Legitimate Python package/MCP server (Apache 2.0) for skill discovery. Has PyPI package (installable via pip install skyll). Clean package structure: skyll/__init__.py, client.py, models.py. Also includes a registry/, skills/, docs/, web/, tests/ directory. Dockerfile and fly.toml indicate it runs as a hosted service. The tool makes REST API calls to skills.sh and GitHub to fetch skill content — external network access is expected and documented. No hidden hooks or install scripts. Source is well-organized with proper Python packaging (pyproject.toml). The .agents/ directory at root is an interesting pattern but expected for the project type. Main concern is that it makes outbound network requests to aggregate skills, but this is the stated purpose and behavior is transparent. No dangerous patterns identified.
